content.full_text:
Oct 26 | 17FH 04                                                    4-84-275


                    Town of Yountville
                                                           October 24, 1984


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Gentlemen:

Enclosed please find an excerpt from the October, 1984 Western City issue
relative to Officeholders not involved in an Election.

I request a clarification on this matter. Our Town Council consists of five
members - four Councilmembers elected for four year terms, and a Mayor elected
for a two year term. Other than election reporting requirements, I have only
had the Councilmembers file an annual report for the period of taking office
through December 31st or January 1st through December 31st, which ever is
applicable.

As of July 1, 1984, the five member Council receives a salary of $150.00 per
month each. Does this mean that they will now have to file a "semi-annual"
campaign statement? Non of my Councilmembers have mid election funds to
report. If this form is required, what is the Form No. and what would be the
period covered? Would they still be required to file annual disclosure
statements?

Your assistance in this matter will be greatly appreciated.

11/9/84--Called, left message. She's on vacation                Sincerely,
for three weeks.
12/3--Discussed filing procedures for officeholders    [illegible]
not being voted on. She was confused between           Frances E. Field
721 and campaign filings.                              Town Clerk

C. Wardlow


                              TOWN HALL
                    6550 Yount Street, P.O. Box 2590
                 Yountville, California 94599 (707) 944-8851

not. Taxable earnings include interest
earned on bank deposits, gains on the
sale of assets and other similar items of
income. (For further clarification, con-
tact the Internal Revenue Service and the
Franchise Tax Board.)

When to File

Following are general guidelines for
when campaign statements must be filed
for the last half of 1984.

Officeholders Not Involved in an Elec-
tion — If you are an officeholder not up
for re-election during July 1 through De-
cember 31, 1984, and your salary is
$100 or more per month, you are re-
quired to file a "semi-annual" campaign
statement regardless of whether you have
received any contributions or made any
expenditures. Due January 31, 1985,
this statement covers the period begin-
ning with the day after the closing date
of the last statement you filed (July 1,
1984, for most filers). If you have not
raised or spent $500 or more during the
entire calendar year 1984, you may file
the "Candidate and Officeholder Cam-
paign Statement — Short Form" (Form
470). In addition to the semi-annual
statement, you may be required to file
a campaign statement during what is
called a threshold period (see box titled

Standard Filing Schedule) if you raise or
spend $500 or more and also make a con-
tribution to another candidate or ballot
measure committee during the threshold
period.

Officeholders and Candidates In-
volved in the November Election — If
you are involved in the November elec-
tion, you will be required to file a thresh-
old statement, if you raise or spend $500
or more during a threshold period.

Important Information about
"Late Contributions" — October 21
through November 5, 1984, is the "late
contribution" period for the November
6th General Election. A late contribution
is any contribution, including a loan, ag-
gregating $1,000 or more during the late
contribution period from a single source
that is made to or received by a candi-
date, controlled committee, or commit-
tee primarily formed to support or
oppose a candidate or measure being
voted upon. Both recipients and the can-
didate or committee making the late con-
tribution must file reports within 48
hours of either receiving or making the
late contribution. A late contribution
report must be sent by telegram,
mailgram, any overnight guaranteed
delivery service or by personal delivery
— not by regular mail.

Standard Filing Schedule for November Elections

Type of Statement          Period Covered               Filing Deadline

September Threshold       July 1-September 17          September 22

October Threshold         September 18-October 20      October 25

Semi-Annual              The day after the closing date of the    January 31, 1985
                         last statement OR January 1 if no
                         previous statement has been filed
                         through December 31.

These are the dates campaign statements are due for officeholders not involved
in an election from July 1 through December 31 and for candidates who are involved
in the November election.

Filing Schedule for April 9, 1985, Election

Type of Statement          Period Covered               Filing Deadline

1st Pre-Election          January 1-February 23        February 28

2nd Pre-Election          February 24-March 23         March 28

Post-Election             March 24-June 6              June 13

Officeholders and Candidates In-
volved in the April 9, 1985, Election —
The following filing schedule is for can-
didates and committees involved in an
April 9, 1985, election. There are no
"thresholds" for statements filed in con-
nection with elections held on dates other
than the June or November state election
dates. Pre- and post-election campaign
statements must be filed during each
period, regardless of whether there has
been any reportable activity. If you an-
ticipate receiving or spending less than
$500 on your entire campaign, you may
avoid filing pre- and post-election sta-
tements by filing a Form 470 with your
Declaration of Candidacy or filing the
Form 470 on or before the deadline for
the first pre-election campaign state-
ment. The period covered by the Form
470 would be January 1, 1985, through
June 30, 1985. Candidates who antici-
pate raising or spending $500 or more
on behalf of your candidate should not
use the Form 470, but file according to
the election schedule for the April 9
election.

March 24 through April 8 is the "late
contribution" period for an April 9th
election.■

[Black box containing advertisement]

Municipal
Finance
Corporation

Specialists in Municipal
Lease-Purchase

Private placements of Certificate
Participation for civic projects of
capital equipment acquisitions.

Construction Projects
Computers
Telephone Systems
Heavy Equipment
Etc.

4045 Bonita Rd., Ste. 308
Bonita, CA 92002
(619) 475-1524
Jerry Jeremiah

717 "K" Street, Ste. 217
Sacramento, CA 95814
(916) 441-5880
William Slaton

Western City/October 1984                                                11

sections.question: Does this mean that they will now have to file a "semi-annual" campaign statement? Non of my Councilmembers have mid election funds to report. If this form is required, what is the Form No. and what would be the period covered? Would they still be required to file annual disclosure statements?
sections.question_synthetic: The Town Clerk of Yountville seeks clarification on whether council members receiving $150/month salary are now required to file semi-annual campaign statements under new FPPC guidelines, and if so, what form number and reporting periods apply.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response/advice letter. No formal CONCLUSION or SHORT ANSWER section provided. Internal FPPC notes dated 11/9/84 and 12/3/84 indicate staff follow-up but no formal response text is included in this document. The enclosed Western City article discusses filing requirements for officeholders not involved in elections but does not constitute an FPPC formal response.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request from the Town Clerk of Yountville, California, seeking clarification on FPPC filing requirements for five-member town council members whose salaries increased to $150/month as of July 1, 1984. The request includes a Western City article excerpt about officeholder filing requirements and internal FPPC notes from follow-up calls and discussions.
